titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Harold Jackson Gordon Jr. Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Manuscripts and Archives Staff

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 13, 2021 at 08:01

did.abstract: Correspondence from civilians and former army officers in Germany relating to Gordon's dissertation: The Reichswehr and the German Republic, 1919-1926 (Yale University, 1953). The letters from the civilians written between 1946 and 1951 discuss their recollections of the Nazi movement and contemporary economic and political conditions in Germany. The letters on the German army, responding to a questionnaire sent by Gordon are from Germany army officers, a British general, a German member of the French resistance and others with information on the army and foreign office personnel policies. Also included are a biographical card file and correspondence, documents, and writings relating to the Hitler Putsch of 1923, to the Beer Hall Putsch, and to the Hamburg KDP Revolt of 1923.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Harold J. Gordon, Jr., 1952. Gift of Nancy M. Gordon 1983 and 2000. Gift of Michael B. Barrett, 2018.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by the Donor and her heirs and assignees of this collection for materials authored or otherwise produced by Harold J. Gordon, Jr. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Harold Jackson Gordon Jr. Papers (MS 246). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Harold Jackson Gordon (1919-1980): professor and author; history professor, University of Massachusetts, Amherst, 1955-1962; United States Army, 1943-1946; United States Army Reserve, 1946-1973; author, The Reichswehr and the German Republic, 1919-1926, Hitler and the Beer Hall Putsch; also contributor to Military Affairs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence from civilians and former army officers in Germany relating to Gordon's dissertation: The Reichswehr and the German Republic, 1919-1926 (Yale University, 1953). The letters from the civilians written between 1946 and 1951 discuss their recollections of the Nazi movement and contemporary economic and political conditions in Germany. The letters on the German army, responding to a questionnaire sent by Gordon are from Germany army officers, a British general, a German member of the French resistance and others with information on the army and foreign office personnel policies. Also included are a biographical card file and correspondence, documents, and writings relating to the Hitler Putsch of 1923, to the Beer Hall Putsch, and to the Hamburg KDP Revolt of 1923.

did.unittitle: Proceedings of the committee on personnel policies of the German Foreign Office
Research notes on German Foreign Office personnel

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Received from Mrs. H. J. Gordon, 1983 Apr 28.

did.unittitle: Correspondence regarding Reichswehr personnel matters from personnel officers and general staff officers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The addition includes correspondence, testimonies, and other research materials Gordon collected to support his research on Reichswehr Germany. The first folder contains an item level inventory, compiled by Nancy M. Gordon, of all materials in the addition. Folder titles correspond to those used in Gordon's itemized listing.

did.unittitle: Testimonies of German officers concerning Reichswehr

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This addition consists of index cards containing biographical information of early members of the National Socialist German Workers Party (NSDAP), Freikorps members, police officers, figures on the political left, and others during the Weimar Republic and later.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The original titles and arrangement used by Gordon have been retained. Within files, cards are arranged alphabetically or divided by sub-topic.

did.unittitle: National Socialist German Workers' Party (NSDAP) I and II, 1919-1924
